title: status --json summarises lanes by default; full records behind a flag (P2)
action: USER-REPORTED: `bee status --json` returns ~4000 tokens (measured 16,017 bytes on this repo) and the `lanes` block alone is 9,249 of them — 58%. AGENTS.md startup step 3 mandates `status --json` at EVERY session start and after EVERY compaction, so this is a per-session context tax that costs far more than its 277-290ms latency. Make `lanes` summarise by default: the ACTIVE lane's full record (that is what a session actually needs), plus counts by phase for the rest, plus the ids — and put the full per-lane records behind an explicit flag (`--lanes-full`, or `--full`; follow whatever flag idiom the registry already uses, and register it). Apply the same judgement to `recent_decisions` only if it is trivially safe — otherwise leave it and note it. The DEFAULT payload must stay a strict subset in meaning: every field a consumer needs to route (phase, mode, feature, gates, cells, recommended_next) is untouched; only the historical lane records move behind the flag. RED-FIRST: write the checks first (default payload contains the active lane in full + counts and NOT the historical records; --lanes-full restores them byte-equal to today's shape; a consumer reading phase/mode/feature/gates/cells/recommended_next sees no change), run red, record --passed false, then implement. Measure and report the before/after byte count of `status --json` on this repo. TARGETED verify only (the status/CLI suites).

truths: default status --json no longer carries historical lane records; the active lane stays complete
phase/mode/feature/gates/cells/recommended_next are byte-unchanged in the default payload
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never drop a field a router needs from the default payload
Do not change what status MEANS — this is payload size only

trace.outcome: status --json default payload 17.9KB -> 4.6KB (~1145 tokens): lanes summarise to active+counts+ids, full records behind --lanes-full; every routing field byte-identical
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":["test_cli_state.mjs lanes assertions (asserted the old full-array default and had to be rewritten)"],"tests_added":["4 checks in test_bee_cli.mjs: default summary shape, --lanes-full restores the legacy array, routing fields byte-identical across both forms, flag registered with a runnable example; plus test_cli_state.mjs rewritten to cover both forms with a CLEAN_ENV fix so ambient session ids cannot shadow the fixture identity"],"red_failure_evidence":"The 4 new checks failed against unmodified code (229 passed / 4 failed, trace --passed false) — status --json emitted the full historical lane array by default, which is the 58%-of-payload condition the user measured at ~4000 tokens on a clean checkout.","verification_run":"Worker: 233/233 test_bee_cli, 54/54 test_cli_state, byte counts 17,854 -> 6,343. Orchestrator re-measured on the live repo: default 4,582 bytes (~1,145 tokens) vs --lanes-full 15,904, and verified phase/mode/feature/gates/cells/recommended_next are byte-identical between the two forms. Full chain green 3 consecutive runs (60 suites). Note: the lpsp-1 ledger guard caught the worker's own .bee/bin edit as ledger-stale on its first real use, forcing a re-onboard before green — the guard works."}
